description: Letter from the thirteenth century. An Alexandrian cantor named Berakhot writes to a physician and poet named Yedutun (Psalms 39:1) in Fustat. (This Yedutun may or may not be identical with the well known poet and cantor Yedutun ha-Levi from the same time period.) The two used to belong to a circle of friends. The letter reflects the activity of such a cycle of friends which counted cantors, physicians and Parnasim (welfare officials) among its members. (Information from Frenkel; see also Goitein, Med. Soc. vol. 5, pp.285-6)
description_he: מכתב מהמאה ה-13. חזן אלכסנדרוני בשם ברכות כותב לרופא ומשורר בשם ידותון (תהלים לט:א) בפוסטאט. שניהם השתייכו למעגל חברים שכלל חזנים, רופאים ופרנסים.
